blocks.text: Teaching Overload (TO) refers to actual teaching load in

excess of the 24 units that will be paid honoraria/overtime pay subject to existing policies thereof and subject further to availability of funds. Teaching overload is treated as overtime if rendered after office hours on a scheduled workday, on a rest day or scheduled day off, holiday, or special non-working day. The maximum TO that may be compensated is 10 full units equivalent to 10 teaching hours or as provided in the programs, standards, and guidelines of their respective programs. In cases where funds are not sufficient, only a percentage of TO may be paid as determined by the available fund. Any excess units not compensated due to unavailability of funds are converted to service credits.
Workload (WL) refers to loads of work given to faculty
members per term that includes assignments/ functions in a) instruction, b) research, c) extension, d) production/ IGP
and e) others/administrative designations. The regular WL of a faculty in Jose Rizal Memorial State University is 24 units.
Section 1 Academic teaching positions shall be classified in
accordance with academic qualifications, training and

scholarship preferably into Professor, Associate Professor, Assistant Professor, and Instructor, without prejudice to a more simplified or expanded system of faculty ranking, at the option of the institution (CMO 40,

An academic teaching personnel, who does not fall under any of the classes or ranks indicated in the preceding paragraph shall be classified preferably as professorial lecturer, guest lecturer, or any other similar academic designation on the basis of his qualifications (CMO 40,
Section 2 The ‘lecturer’ designation is given to a non-regular
member of the teaching staff who is paid on hourly basis.
Lecturer may be renewed on a semester basis.
Depending on their qualifications, lecturers may be given

fixed_blocks.text:
4 Teaching Overload (TO) refers to actual teaching load in excess of the 24 units that will be paid honoraria/overtime pay subject to existing policies thereof and subject further to availability of funds. Teaching overload is treated as overtime if rendered after office hours on a scheduled workday, on a rest day or scheduled day off, holiday, or special non-working day. The maximum TO that may be compensated is 10 full units equivalent to 10 teaching hours or as provided in the programs, standards, and guidelines of their respective programs. In cases where funds are not sufficient, only a percentage of TO may be paid as determined by the available fund. Any excess units not compensated due to unavailability of funds are converted to service credits.

Workload (WL) refers to loads of work given to faculty members per term that includes assignments/ functions in a) instruction, b) research, c) extension, d) production/ IGP and e) others/administrative designations. The regular WL of a faculty in Jose Rizal Memorial State University is 24 units.

IV. Faculty Classification and Ranking

Section 1 Academic teaching positions shall be classified in accordance with academic qualifications, training and scholarship preferably into Professor, Associate Professor, Assistant Professor, and Instructor, without prejudice to a more simplified or expanded system of faculty ranking at the option of the institution (CMO 40, s.2008; CMO 30, s.2009). An academic teaching personnel who does not fall under any of the classes or ranks indicated in the preceding paragraph shall be classified preferably as professorial lecturer, guest lecturer, or any other similar academic designation on the basis of his qualifications (CMO 40, s.2008; CMO 30, s.2009). Section 2 The ‘lecturer’ designation is given to a non-regular member of the teaching staff who is paid on hourly basis. Lecturer may be renewed on a semester basis. Depending on their qualifications, lecturers may be given

qa_pairs.question: What is teaching overload in JRMSU?
qa_pairs.answer: Teaching overload in JRMSU refers to actual teaching load in excess of the 24 units that will be paid honoraria/overtime pay subject to existing policies thereof and subject further to availability of funds.

qa_pairs.question: What is the regular workload of a faculty member in JRMSU?
qa_pairs.answer: The regular workload of a faculty member in JRMSU is 24 units, which includes assignments/functions in instruction, research, extension, production/IGP, and others/administrative designations.

An academic teaching personnel who does not fall under any of the classes or ranks indicated in the preceding paragraph shall be classified preferably as professorial lecturer, guest lecturer, or any other similar academic designation on the basis of his qualifications (CMO 40, s.2008; CMO 30, s.2009). Section 2 The ‘lecturer’ designation is given to a non-regular member of the teaching staff who is paid on hourly basis. Lecturer may be renewed on a semester basis. Depending on their qualifications, lecturers may be given
qa_pairs.question: What is the maximum workload that can be assigned to faculty members in JRMSU?
qa_pairs.answer: The maximum workload that can be assigned to faculty members in JRMSU is 24 units.

qa_pairs.context:
4 Teaching Overload (TO) refers to actual teaching load in excess of the 24 units that will be paid honoraria/overtime pay subject to existing policies thereof and subject further to availability of funds. Teaching overload is treated as overtime if rendered after office hours on a scheduled workday, on a rest day or scheduled day off, holiday, or special non-working day. The maximum TO that may be compensated is 10 full units equivalent to 10 teaching hours or as provided in the programs, standards, and guidelines of their respective programs. In cases where funds are not sufficient, only a percentage of TO may be paid as determined by the available fund. Any excess units not compensated due to unavailability of funds are converted to service credits.

Workload (WL) refers to loads of work given to faculty members per term that includes assignments/ functions in a) instruction, b) research, c) extension, d) production/ IGP and e) others/administrative designations. The regular WL of a faculty in Jose Rizal Memorial State University is 24 units.

Section 2 The ‘lecturer’ designation is given to a non-regular member of the teaching staff who is paid on hourly basis. Lecturer may be renewed on a semester basis. qa_pairs.question: How are funds allocated for workload in JRMSU?
qa_pairs.answer: Funds for workload in JRMSU are allocated based on the available fund, and only a percentage of the workload may be paid as determined by the institution.

qa_pairs.answer: Excess workload in JRMSU is converted to service credits.
